steps.title: Combine constant terms
steps.content: In the expression $8+d^{2}+3$, identify the constant terms (8 and 3) and the variable term ($d^{2}$). Add the constants together: $8+3=11$.

steps.title: Write the simplified expression
steps.content: The variable term $d^{2}$ remains unchanged, and the combined constants give us $d^{2}+11$. That matches choice B — B is correct.
